description: BitBio Pixel Art Quality Rubric. Each asset must be scored before shipping. Minimum 7.0 to ship. Target 8.5.

silhouetteReadability.description: Can you identify the sprite/tile at blur, grayscale, or 25% scale?

scores.0: Completely unreadable at any reduction. Looks like abstract noise.
scores.3: Barely readable. Requires full-color full-size to identify.
scores.5: Readable at full size, loses identity when blurred or scaled down.
scores.7: Readable at 50% scale. Identity clear in grayscale.
scores.9: Readable at 25% scale. Iconic silhouette. Could be a logo.
scores.10: Frisk, Papyrus, Samus tier — instantly iconic at any resolution.

silhouetteReadability.testMethod: Apply 5px Gaussian blur + convert to grayscale. If character is identifiable: 7+. If character AND emotion readable: 9+.

colorHarmony.description: Does the palette use hue-shifting? Are colors intentionally related?

scores.0: Random colors, no visible palette discipline.
scores.3: Flat darkening only. Shadows are just darker versions of base with no hue shift.
scores.5: Some palette discipline but colors clash or feel unrelated.
scores.7: Clear hue-shifted ramps. Shadows cool-shifted, highlights warm-shifted.
scores.9: Palette tells a story. Every color feels inevitable, not accidental.
scores.10: Sea of Stars / Eastward quality — professional color artist level.

colorHarmony.testMethod: Check: does shadow shift toward blue/purple? Does highlight shift toward yellow/orange? If yes to both: 7+.

realmConsistency.description: Does the asset use only its realm's canonical palette from BITBIO-PALETTE.json?

scores.0: Uses colors from 3+ different realms. Looks like it belongs to a different game.
scores.3: Has some realm colors but several out-of-realm additions.
scores.5: Mostly realm-correct but has 2-3 off-palette colors.
scores.7: All colors within realm palette ± tolerance of 30 color distance.
scores.9: Perfect realm compliance AND contributes to realm identity.
scores.10: Asset that defines the realm's visual identity going forward.

realmConsistency.testMethod: Run validateRealmAsset() from style-checker.ts. Score < 80% compliance = max score 5.

readabilityAtGameScale.description: At S=3 scale on a 1512px-wide screen, does the asset serve its gameplay role clearly?

scores.0: Asset is invisible or indistinguishable from background at game scale.
scores.3: Barely visible. Color shape only, no recognizable details.
scores.5: Recognizable category (character, wall, floor) but not specific identity.
scores.7: Identity clear. Player can tell NPC from player, floor from wall.
scores.9: Identity + state readable (which NPC, which lesson node, interactive vs non-interactive).
scores.10: Every pixel visible at game scale serves a purpose.

readabilityAtGameScale.testMethod: Screenshot the actual game. Zoom to 100% actual CSS pixels. Is the asset serving its role?

animationPolish.description: For animated assets: does motion feel weighted, alive, and game-appropriate?

scores.3: 2-frame toggle with no weight or personality.
scores.5: Functional 2-4 frame animation. Movement is conveyed but feels mechanical.
scores.7: Natural-feeling animation. Character identity maintained across frames.
scores.9: Anticipation, follow-through, or sub-pixel polish visible.
scores.10: Stardew Valley / OMORI walk cycle quality — every frame delightful.

animationPolish.testMethod: Run scoreWalkCycle() from animation-scorer.ts. Overall score < 75 = rubric score ≤ 6.

styleConsistency.description: Does the asset match the established BitBio visual style? Would it look at home in the game?

scores.0: Completely different style — looks like it came from another game.
scores.3: Some style elements match, but feels like a different artist's work.
scores.5: Matches the general dark aesthetic but has inconsistencies.
scores.7: Fits naturally into existing game scenes. Indistinguishable from existing assets.
scores.9: Elevates the visual style of assets around it.
scores.10: Sets the new standard for the game's visual style.

styleConsistency.testMethod: Place asset in a screenshot next to existing game assets. Does it look like it belongs?

currentBitBio.note: Assessed June 2026. Zone identity excellent. Sprite size and color ramps need most work.

decisionTree.score_below_5: REJECT — Do not ship. Return to design phase. Document what failed.
decisionTree.score_5_to_7: REVISE — Ship temporarily to unblock other work. Create backlog ticket to improve.
decisionTree.score_7_to_8_5: SHIP — Meets standard. Document for future improvement but not blocking.
decisionTree.score_above_8_5: FEATURE — This asset sets the quality bar. Reference it in DESIGN-RULES.md.
decisionTree.below_blocking_minimum: CRITICAL BLOCK — Even if overall score is 7+, asset cannot ship if silhouette or readability is below 5.0.

reviewProcess: 1. Run automated tools: validateRealmAsset() + scoreWalkCycle() if animated
2. Score each rubric category manually using the testMethod
6. Document score in asset's commit message: [QA: 7.8/10]
7. If shipping with score < 8.5, create GitHub issue for improvement
